title: Petit, the Poet
content:
Seeds in a dry pod, tick, tick, tick,
        Tick, tick, tick, like mites in a quarrel - 
        Faint iambics that the full breeze wakens - 
        But the pine tree makes a symphony thereof.
        Triolets, villanelles, rondels, rondeaus,
        Ballades by the score with the same old thought:
        The snows and the roses of yesterday are vanished;
        And what is love but a rose that fades?
        Life all around me here in the village:
        Tragedy, comedy, valor and truth,
        Courage, constancy, heroism, failure - 
        All in the loom, and oh what patterns!
        Woodlands, meadows, streams and rivers - 
        Blind to all of it all my life long.
        Triolets, villanelles, rondels, rondeaus,
        Seeds in a dry pod, tick, tick, tick, Tick, tick, tick, what little iambics,
        While Homer and Whitman roared in the pines?
meaning:
Edgar Lee Masters's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Petit, the Poet"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
